speakers.name: Vishal Alhat
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio:
A Developer Advocate at Amazon Web Services (AWS), he focuses on empowering builders and scaling technical communities across Asia Pacific, Japan, and China. With over a decade of experience in software development, DevOps, and cybersecurity, he brings a strong practitioner’s perspective to developer advocacy. His approach is rooted in deep technical empathy—understanding real-world developer challenges and championing their success within AWS by bridging the gap between builders and cloud services.

His journey is strongly grounded in community leadership, having been recognized as an AWS Hero and AWS Community Builder of the Year. He has led and contributed to prominent user groups including AWS, HashiCorp, and MongoDB, and continues to shape the ecosystem through technical content, international speaking engagements, and active mentorship. Passionate about DevRel, FinOps, and cybersecurity best practices, he is driven by a strong sense of ownership and a personal belief in integrity—ensuring that developer voices meaningfully influence the future of cloud innovation.

speakers.name: Jatin Mehrotra
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio:
A DevOps and Cloud Engineer with 5+ years of experience, he currently serves as a Developer Advocate at Amazon Web Services (AWS), where he empowers developers and drives community engagement around modern cloud practices. His expertise lies in designing, automating, and scaling secure cloud-native infrastructure across AWS and Microsoft Azure.

His work spans platform engineering, CI/CD automation, observability, and cost optimization, with a growing focus on MLOps and Generative AI. He has built and managed production-grade systems using Terraform, AWS CDK, Kubernetes (EKS/AKS), and modern CI/CD tools like GitHub Actions and Argo CD. Beyond his role at AWS, he is an active community contributor and cloud evangelist, known for sharing practical, real-world insights through blogs, live sessions, and speaking engagements. A multi-time AWS Community Builder and certified professional, he has authored 50+ technical articles and mentored aspiring engineers in cloud and DevOps. Passionate about solving meaningful engineering problems, he brings a hands-on, pragmatic approach to building scalable, secure, and cost-efficient systems while continuously exploring the evolving landscape of AI-driven engineering.

speakers.name: Shubham Londhe
speakers.title: Sr. Developer Advocate
speakers.bio:
Shubham is a seasoned backend and platform engineer with 8+ years of experience building and scaling production systems across global companies including Temporal (US), AWS India, Liven (Australia), and Vamstar (UK). His work spans backend architecture, DevOps systems, and platform engineering, with a strong focus on reliability, scalability, and real-world production challenges.

Beyond industry, Shubham is the creator of TrainWithShubham, India’s largest DevOps learning community. Over the past 5 years, he has guided thousands of engineers through structured roadmaps and career transitions into cloud roles. In his session, you can expect real-world DevOps and platform engineering lessons from production systems, clear career roadmaps for both freshers and experienced engineers, and deep insights into modern hiring patterns. He also explores the practical use of Generative AI and Agentic AI in modern engineering workflows. His insights are grounded in over 2,500+ learner journeys, offering a rare blend of industry depth and teaching clarity. Shubham continues to shape the ecosystem through his YouTube channel and dedicated cohort programs at trainwithshubham.com.

speakers.name: Nasiullha Chaudhari
speakers.title: Developer Relations | Docker Captain
speakers.bio:
Nasiullha Chaudhari is a DevOps and Cloud Engineering leader known for driving automation, scalable infrastructure, and cloud-native transformation across modern organizations. Recognized as a HashiCorp Ambassador and Docker Captain, he is an active voice in the global DevOps ecosystem, contributing through conferences, technical content, and open-source initiatives.

He has collaborated with leading organizations including AWS, Docker, Grafana, the Linux Foundation, HashiCorp, and Twingate, helping advance best practices in cloud and platform engineering. With deep expertise across DevOps, GitOps, and multi-cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP), Nasiullha specializes in building resilient systems using tools like Kubernetes, Terraform, ArgoCD, and Jenkins. His work spans infrastructure as code, containerization, monitoring, and security, enabling teams to operate efficiently at scale. As an educator and mentor, he has guided countless engineers through hands-on learning and real-world insights, shaping careers and strengthening the cloud-native community.

speakers.name: Ashish Kumar
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio:
A Cloud and DevOps Specialist, he is known for designing scalable, secure, and cost-efficient cloud solutions that help organizations unlock the true potential of modern infrastructure. With deep expertise in Amazon Web Services (AWS) and DevOps practices, he focuses on infrastructure automation, performance optimization, and building resilient systems.

His work consistently emphasizes a business-first approach—aligning cloud strategy with real outcomes, from reducing operational overhead to enabling faster innovation. Beyond his technical contributions, he is an active community leader and knowledge advocate. As an organizer with CNCG New Delhi and an AWS Community Builder, he plays a key role in fostering collaboration and learning within the cloud ecosystem. Known for his ability to simplify complex challenges, he has helped teams significantly optimize cloud costs, automate delivery pipelines, and strengthen system performance and security. His mission is clear: empower teams to build smarter, scale faster, and innovate without limits.
